RaceName: Prix du Clos de la Barre (Conditions) (Turf)
Spotlight: Just one win in 11 starts but some notable form in defeat includes a second to last week's Ascot Gold Cup runner Caballo De Mar in a 1m6f Group 3; good third in a Listed race last time over 1m2f; considered.

RaceName: Prix du Clos de la Barre (Conditions) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mme Genevieve Meunier, Elevage Des Vallons Et Al
Spotlight: Only 2-18 but ran a number of good races in defeat last year, including a 2l defeat in a 1m4f Group 2; made an encouraging return when beaten 4.5l in a Listed race over 1m2f, a trip that was on the sharp side.

RaceName: Prix du Clos de la Barre (Conditions) (Turf)
Spotlight: A four-time winner but thoroughly exposed now after 25 races and has an air of vulnerability about him against one or two of these.

RaceName: Prix du Clos de la Barre (Conditions) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has won nine of his 33 races but a 7yo now and needs to step up on this season's form; finished behind Waldnebel last month.

RaceName: Prix du Clos de la Barre (Conditions) (Turf)
Spotlight: Listed winner (1m4f, good to soft) who has held his own in a Group 2 and was winning for a fourth time when odds-on for a conditions race at Saint-Cloud in early May (won by 6l; 1m2f, good); every chance.

RaceName: Prix du Clos de la Barre (Conditions) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won at two and added a Listed race (1m6f, very soft) last year at Longchamp in September; posted a similar RPR as that day on her reappearance but latest run was underwhelming, trailing home last; possibilities if back to her best.

Half-brother to Daryz; tongue tied for both his races; odds-on when impressing in a newcomers race at Saint-Cloud (1m, soft) and for one so inexperienced he did well to be only 3l off Constitution River in the Group 1 Prix du Jockey Club (C&D, good), especially as he had to be dropped from stall ten; remains a very smart prospect and this looks his to lose.
Sole win in seven races came over 6f in October (good to soft), since when he's had limitations exposed in two Listed races and two conditions events (7f-1m2f); looks very vulnerable.

Raven's Pass gelding who has completed a hat-trick in 7f/1m races at Prague; it's difficult to get a firm angle on his form but he's clearly talented and an interesting runner from the Czech Republic.

OwnerName: Ecu Normandy Spirit, Ecu La Vallee Martigny & G Pelisson
Spotlight: Came good in his fifth race, a 1m2f Longchamp handicap over 1m2f a month ago (good to soft); he's in a lot deeper here and has plenty to find.
Beat a well-bred newcomer with the pair 2l clear in a 1m2f maiden on good to soft at Compiegne; that form has yet to undergo a thorough test but it was a promising start.
Won his second race but has just under 2l to find with Deal on their Angers clash earlier this month (1m, good to soft) and there are others to worry about.

OwnerName: Cormac Farrell, William Jackson-Stops & Monaughty Bloodstock
Spotlight: After winning on very soft at Compiegne (1m) he was beaten just over a length in a Listed race at Toulouse (good to soft); he just never gave his running at Saint-Cloud a month ago (1m handicap; 4-1); could be worth another chance.

Justified favouritism over the extended 1m1f (AW) here in November, winning easily; down the field since then, with the Group 2 defeat excusable but he was then well beaten in a conditions race; still early days, though.
Quite strong in the betting for all his races and had a nice bit in hand in a maiden here in May (1m, very soft); holes can be picked in that form but he's looking progressive.

No Nay Never colt with winning form and he had Mister Charmes behind when going down narrowly to a useful rival (who wasn't beaten far in a Listed race the run before) over 1m at Angers (good to soft); others in here have achieved that bit more.

Won over a mile at Lyon Parilly (soft) before proving no match for the odds-on Repel at Nancy (7f); the winner finished well beaten in last week's Sandringham at Royal Ascot; this filly appears to be heading the right way.
Took six goes to get off the mark, prevailing narrowly in a 1m maiden at Compiegne (good to soft); unlikely that a repeat of that would see her follow up here.

Both runs at 1m; narrow winner on heavy ground at Deauville first time out and unlikely that she gave her true running when beating one home at Longchamp (good to soft); well-bred daughter of Dubawi who retains potential.
Both runs over around 7f at Saint-Cloud; beaten 9l on heavy first time out but then edged home in a tight finish to a maiden on soft; more needed here but that's feasible.

Won C&D Group 2 (heavy) in 2024 and this 8yo has won two 5f Listed races this year, in the UAE (AW) and Italy (good); runner-up in Group 3 over C&D (good) latest and this front-runner can make a bold bid despite 5lb penalty.

Went very close in two warm conditions races at Fontainebleu (5.5f, very soft) in March, behind Lesslepasser on the first occasion, and he was denied a clear run for a considerable spell when fifth in Group 3 at Longchamp (5f, soft) last month; interesting, granted better luck.

Arrives with something to prove having been below par in seventh on his two outings this year; however, he was a close second in the Group 1 Prix de l'Abbaye at Longchamp (5f, very soft) last October and will hold leading claims if rediscovering that form.

Beat Estepona by a nose in conditions race at Fontainebleu (5.5f, very soft) on his reappearance; however, he's failed to kick on subsequently and needs to settle better than when last of five over C&D last time.

Third for Ralph Beckett in Group 3 at Ascot (6f, good to firm) in 2yo campaign in 2024; lightly raced since (including stint in US) and has something to find in today's company but she made a big move to dispute the lead 1f out on last month's stable and seasonal debut here (6f, heavy), before fading into fourth; intriguing contender on this first crack at a bare 5f.

Lightly raced 4yo who was progressive last season and continued the good work with a conditions race win here (6f, good) on her reappearance; she's better than she showed when a Listed fifth here (6f, good to soft) last time and it's possible the drop to 5f will be a positive; a possible.

3yo who was second to subsequent King Charles III runner-up Rayevka in a Group 3 at Longchamp (5f, soft) last month and got no run in the closing stages when close fourth of five in Group 3 over this C&D last time (good; Ponntos second); lots to like provided he gets the breaks this time.
2yo winner at Deauville (6.5f, AW) last December and he's performed respectably among his four starts this year, but needs something extra today.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ten-time winner, most recently on AW (8.5f) in December; also suited by turf but needs to up his game after some disappointing recent efforts.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Must bounce back from a poor run a fortnight ago and has not won since 2024 but was placed off today's mark on consecutive 1m turf starts in April/May and has obvious claims if judged on those efforts.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has modest 1-29 strike-rate but was placed on Polytrack here (9.5f) in April and also ran well when close sixth over that C&D last month; should also be suited by this trip-and-surface combination; high on the list.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Just 2-46 and was behind Rubis Gold when sixth at Compiegne (1m, heavy) last month but won this race off a slightly higher mark a year ago (good ground) and could be dangerous if perked up by recent stable switch.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ended 2025 with 7.5f AW win but is difficult to enthuse over after three down-the-field turf runs this spring.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won on Polytrack here (1m) in October; absent since finishing well from the rear for sixth on that surface in December (7.5f) but this return to turf won't be a problem; claims if ready to roll.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: 20-race maiden who struggled to get competitive on his last two appearances (both over 1m on soft ground).

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ran poorly at Compiegne a fortnight ago and remains without a win since September 2024 but his staying-on third at Saint-Cloud (1m, good to soft) in April makes him one of the more interesting candidates in this line-up.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Runner-up at 81-1 odds on AW here (1m) in March but quickly back in the doldrums when back on turf last month; others look much safer.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Regressive mare who has shown very little at triple-digit odds on her recent outings and usually runs over much longer trips.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Plaine (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Seemed to run well when third of five on AW in Belgium (10.5f) last summer but has been out of form this year.
Four wins over 1m/1m1f, but none since September 2024; drops into a Class 5 for the first time, but he didn't get home in one previous attempt over this far which raises doubts.

OwnerName: Graham Kot Li Heng & Amy Chau
Spotlight: 1-17 and his best form to date has come on dirt; has stamina to prove as well.

OwnerName: Lam Ka Lai & Yeung Wing Shut
Spotlight: Made it 15th-time lucky with a convincing success over C&D four weeks ago; 8lb higher but stays in Class 5 company; major player.
0-9, but came closest to winning when runner-up on his dirt debut at Sha Tin (1m1f) 17 days ago; needs to show he can do it back on turf and has to prove his stamina.

OwnerName: Cheung King Shek, Billy Cheung King Shan Et Al
Spotlight: Several creditable efforts this year as when third here three weeks ago, but is just 1-34 in Hong Kong and has a major question to answer on his first attempt beyond an extended 1m.
1-18, the win coming over 7f early last year; needs to prove his stamina, but he has shaped as though he would stay this far on a few occasions; Zac Purton retains the faith.
Never figured on dirt (1m1f) last time, but stamina for this far is no problem having won twice over C&D; just 2lb above his last winning mark and a player.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Jerry Chow Shiu Ming
Spotlight: Record of 1-34 in Hong Kong suggests others are more likely winners, but he has finished runner-up four times in nine starts over C&D; another place on the podium is a real possibility.
Five wins over 1m/1m1f at Sha Tin; first attempt at this trip when beaten 5l into third behind Rosewood Fleetfoot over C&D four weeks ago; should finish closer on 9lb better terms.

OwnerName: Sunny Wong Chi Tat & Wong Tsz Wah
Spotlight: 0-21 and yet to attempt this far, but was doing all his best work late when third over 1m1f here a fortnight ago; one for the shortlist.
1-27; the win coming over 1m2f at Sha Tin in April last year; has run with credit over this far, but will need more in order to take this.
Placed in one of his six starts over C&D, but now 0-37 and hard to fancy from 9lb out of the weights.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Scored over C&D last year and looked suited by combination of drop back to 1m and return to turf when close second at Compiegne (good to soft) this month; remains on a good mark; might be the answer.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won three times over 1m on AW here in November-March but has badly lost his way in recent months and finished last when back on turf a fortnight ago.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Below form and behind King Hartwood on latest outing a fortnight ago and remains without a win since 2024, but lost out only by a nose at Vichy (1m, soft) last month and has good claims if judged on that effort.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed on consecutive 1m AW starts here at start of this year but has not been firing on all cylinders lately and was a fair way behind King Hartwood on latest turf start in April.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Not beaten far when keeping-on sixth at Longchamp (7f, good) on Thursday and will be fine over today's longer trip but this steadily regressive 8yo has a modest strike-rate in recent years.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Finished well for third at Longchamp (7f, very soft) in early May and is now below that mark but has not been at the same level on any of her four starts since.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Has modest 1-25 strike-rate, the win gained on Polytrack (9.5f) in 2024; kept on for never-dangerous third at Longchamp (1m, very soft) last month but was soundly beaten on his latest outing and is not the percentage call.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Still a maiden after seven runs but was placed over 1m on Polytrack here a year ago and shaped as though the return to this trip would suit when sixth over 7f on that surface in March; has usual cheekpieces back on today; each-way claims if fully tuned up after three-month break.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Veteran with 12 wins on his CV, the latest gained at Longchamp (1m, good to soft) in April; placed under Mickael Barzalona at Compiegne (1m1f/1m) on last two outings; usually ridden patiently; likely contender again.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Not that far behind King Hartwood when fifth at Longchamp (1m, good to soft) in April but generally very disappointing since AW win here in December.

RaceName: Prix de l'Allee de la Petite Haie (Handicap) (Turf)
Spotlight: Yet to add to his two heavy-ground wins in October 2024 and recent efforts have been underwhelming; look elsewhere.

OwnerName: Lau Ying Kin & Lau Mok Yuen Kee
Spotlight: C&D winner who again found 1m1f too far when dropped into Class 5 at Sha Tin last time, but could go well back in trip from his good draw; shortlisted.

OwnerName: The Hon & Mrs Kenneth Lau Ip Keung
Spotlight: Held since his sole narrow success from 2lb lower at Sha Tin (1m) on New Year's Day; opposable from his high draw.
1-29 in Hong Kong, the win coming in a Class 4 over C&D a year ago; visor returns, but he needs to improve on recent efforts if he is to get his head back in front.
2-37 in Hong Kong and mainly seen on dirt (both wins) these days; others preferred.
Out of the frame in all 19 starts in Hong Kong and the drop to Class 5 has made little difference; needs plenty more.
0-14 and well held over C&D three weeks ago, but is better drawn this time and his two efforts off higher marks than today prior to that make him a player in this company.
Unplaced in all 12 starts despite a sliding mark and this trip looks right on the edge of his stamina.

OwnerName: Thomas Poon Ho Wing & Poon Chi Keung
Spotlight: 1-33 in Hong Kong, the win coming over C&D in March last year; has made the frame on several other occasions around here, but the outside stall makes things tricky.
1-29 in Hong Kong and may be better suited by further now, but he has been placed three times off much higher marks this year and is well drawn, so a case can be made.
1-23; latest effort over 1m1f here can be forgiven on account of the draw; 5lb lower than when third over C&D in April and he would be a player of able to replicate that form.
0-26; placed a few times over 5f/6f here and although runner-up over this trip at Sha Tin last month, that was on dirt; enough doubts remain.
0-23 in Hong Kong, but only just failed to last home over C&D three weeks ago; races off the same mark and could be given a chance if repeating that effort, though he isn't well drawn.

Overall record of 1-34 and the win was gained at Deauville (9.5, AW) in November 2024; went close at Longchamp (1m, good to soft) in April but he's not easy to predict and was in rear at Compiegne two weeks ago; down the list.

Got back on the scoresheet with an AW win at Chantilly (9.5f) in March; mixed form in three subsequent runs but he wasn't beaten far here last month; will need a good pace back at this trip but he's not ruled out back on turf.

Kept on well for a close second at Deauville (9.5f, AW) in December but she has a patchy record and was always in rear on her reappearance at Longchamp (10.5f, good too soft) two weeks ago; may have needed that run but others are more convincing.

Dual winner on soft/heavy ground (1m) in 2024; finished down the field in his four runs this year including when eighth at Compiegne (1m, good to soft) this month; has something to prove and he needs to get back on track.

Won on AW here (9.5f) last month and she made a bold bid when runner-up on her return visit two weeks later; looks interesting on that form but she didn't really fire back on turf at Compiegne last time and was behind two of today's rivals in this race; mixed messages.

OwnerName: Christopher Doussot, El Sebastien Jesus et al
Spotlight: Two wins at 1m1f (AW/good) last year; ran well when third at Lyon in March but he's not threatened in four subsequent runs; on a dangerous mark but he needs to raise his game.

Two 7f wins on good to soft last year and she's finished placed in her last two starts including a rallying third at Longchamp (1m, soft) three weeks ago; should be in the mix again and she's respected.
His last success was on AW here (9.5f) last November; held in four runs since but he was a creditable fourth of 16 at Compiegne (1m, soft) last time; in the mix if he can back that up.
Runner-up in three of his last four runs including a close call on tacky ground at Compiegne (1m) last time; went down fighting in that race and he should be in the thick of things again.

5yo who bounced back with an eye-catching third at Compiegne two weeks ago; didn't get much luck in the closing stages of that race and he ties in with Pierre Premier on that form; interesting contender.

OwnerName: Robert Collet & Haras Du Logis Saint-Germain
Spotlight: Turf winner as a 3yo and he won three times on AW at Chantilly (1m/9.5f) last year; mixed results in 2026 but he's reached the frame on a few occasions and his front-running fifth here (9.5f, AW) last month was a good effort; dangerous back in trip.
Far from disgraced on a few occasions in Class 3 company since narrowly winning a Class 4 over C&D in April; much respected back down in grade
Handy sort who is 1-23 in Hong Kong; twice went close over C&D in April and had excuses for his latest effort, but he has the widest stall.
Just two appearances at Class 4 company including a win over C&D in May last year, but he didn't appear to have any excuses back down in grade five weeks ago; needs to bounce back.
2-26 in Hong Kong, both wins coming over 6f here; still has to convince over this far and drawn wider than ideal.
0-7 in Hong Kong, but has twice run well over C&D since stepped up in trip, including when a close fourth from a wide draw three weeks ago; better berthed this time and shortlisted.
All three wins have come over C&D, the latest by a neck from a high draw three weeks ago; again drawn wide and now 5lb higher, but he can't be easily dismissed.
Just one piece of worthwhile form in eight starts in Hong Hong; better drawn than he has been lately, but still makes limited appeal.
Three course wins, two over this trip; on a lengthy losing run, but has mainly been running well this year and has dropped to a handy mark; worth a second look.
All four local wins have come over C&D and he's 1lb lower than for the latest in November; ran well from a wide draw when third here five weeks ago; considered.
Out of the frame in four starts over 6f-1m at Sha Tin; swaps cheekpieces for an eyeshield; more needed.

OwnerName: Ngai Man, Amy Wang Hong & Doris Ngai Yan Tung
Spotlight: Four wins over 1m/1m1f here and only just lost out over C&D last month; held on dirt since, but respected back here from the inside stall.
Placed three times from 11 starts but not proving very consistent; still has stamina to prove.
0-6 in Hong Kong, but only beaten a neck when stepped up in trip and dropped to Class 4 over C&D three weeks ago; shortlisted up 1lb.
Has found things tough in Class 3 company since gaining the latest of his four C&D wins in March, but very much respected back at this level.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Boniface Ho Ka Kui
Spotlight: 1-13 in Hong Kong, mainly over shorter trips at Sha Tin; drop in grade is a positive and he did win over this far in Australia.
Dual C&D winner who has finished runner-up in three of his last four starts over C&D, the latest when 5l behind Vivacious Win five weeks ago; 10lb better off, but that may not be enough.
Off the mark at the 11th attempt with an emphatic 5l success over C&D five weeks ago; 10lb higher, but remains in the same class and Zac Purton maintains the partnership.
1-33 in Hong Kong and the drop to Class 4 company hasn't resulted in an upturn in his fortunes; others stronger.
Won narrowly over C&D in April and beaten less than a length into fifth last month; still unexposed after just four starts, but he again hasn't enjoyed much luck with the draw.
Won over this trip on dirt at Sha Tin in April, but held otherwise including three goes on turf; others more convincing.
Won over 1m1f here and at Sha Tin earlier in the year, but last two efforts have been blowouts; may be best watched for now.

OwnerName: Gan Wee Sean, Gary Gan Cheng Chuah & Gavin Gan Cheng Hooi
Spotlight: 2-4 in Australia, but unplaced in eight local starts; did show more when fourth over C&D five weeks ago and holds each-way claims if taking another step forward.

OwnerName: Billie Lam Chi Fan, Lee Shek Tang Et Al
Spotlight: 1-1 in Britain, but hasn't got going at all since arriving in Hong Kong; best watched.
Out of the frame in five starts, though his last two runs over C&D haven't been without some promise; the outside stall is a real downer, though.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Elvis Kwok Kam Chuen, Winkle Lee Wing Ho Et Al
Spotlight: Well down the field at triple-digit odds on all three Hong Kong starts (5f-6f); dropped in grade today but still can't be recommended.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Consistent 5yo who kept on well for second over C&D three weeks ago and has now made the frame on seven of his nine starts since winning here in November; likely to be on the premises again.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won from the front over C&D in April, but was well below that form on next two outings and makes only limited appeal from stall 10.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Lam Yau Pik, Rex Lam Lam & Monica Lam Mung Ni
Spotlight: Led close home over C&D in April and is now 2-5 since rejoining Douglas Whyte this year; well drawn again today and still well handicapped on older form; shortlisted.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Wong Leung Sau Hing & Eddie Wong Ming Chak
Spotlight: Yet to make a splash after five starts in Hong Kong (7f/1m); well drawn today but it's questionable whether this drop back in trip will suit.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner this year; placed off today's mark here last month but wasn't at same level here a fortnight later and stall 11 asks a lot of him today.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Stephen Ho Wai Man, F Ho Ka Chun & D Ho Ka Chung
Spotlight: Still 4lb higher than when winning at Sha Tin in March, but was placed there last month and arguably bettered than performance when fifth from stall 10 last time; well drawn here; one to consider.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Some efforts this year have been encouraging but he was never dangerous (albeit from a wide stall) when 11th at Sha Tin last month and has now been unplaced in all 11 Hong Kong starts; not the percentage call.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: 0-11 in Hong Kong but has made the frame twice over C&D in recent weeks, latterly from stall 10; better drawn and 2lb lower today; might be the answer.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Rotary Club Of Kwai Chung Syndicate
Spotlight: Placed for first time in five-race career when keeping-on third over C&D last month; needs to build on that performance today but that's possible.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Cheng Wan Wai, Sheena Cheng Tsz Yan & Selina Cheng Tsz Ching
Spotlight: Suggested he's ready to exploit a much-reduced mark when close second over C&D last month but this regressive 6yo has the outside stall today.

RaceName: The Racing World 50th Anniversary Cup (Handicap) (Class 4) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Slightly disappointing here three weeks ago and now 0-9, but lost out only by a short head over C&D in April and has obvious claims if judged on that performance.

RaceName: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 90,000gns yearling, 215,000euros 2yo breeze-ups; eighth foal; No Nay Never brother to winners Noble Vow (5f/5.7f; RPR 81) and Angelica Bay (US 1m turf); dam 6.5f and 1m (Listed) 2yo winner; he's travelled from Newmarket to make his debut and could have a big part to play.

RaceName: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Mpr, A & Glo Outlaw & K Quinn
Spotlight: 1,000euros yearling, resold 25,000euros yearling, £70,000 2yo breeze-ups; third foal; dam 5f 2yo winner (RPR 69), half-sister to some talented winners notably Mac Love (7f-1m2f Group 3) and Club Oceanic (useful 1m-1m2f); this Inns Of Court newcomer could give a good account.

RaceName: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 150,000gns foal, 320,000gns yearling; eighth foal; Havana Grey sister to two winners including Symbology (Group-placed 6f 2yo), closely related to 5f-7f winner El Caballo (including 2yo/Group 2), half-sister to two Italian 2yo winners; dam 5f/6f winner (including 2yo; RPR 91); bred to be a classy juvenile and she has to be of interest on debut.

RaceName: Get Best Odds With Oddschecker Maiden Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 7-2 when fifth of ten on debut at Nottingham (5f, good) three weeks ago, beaten just over 4l by the winner Senorita Bonita who was runner-up at Royal Ascot last week; this 305,000euros yearling has an attractive pedigree and is open to improvement for a top trainer who won this last year with one taking a big step forward on her second start.

OwnerName: Fong Chung Man, Vincent Fong & Lucia Fong So Ching
Spotlight: Yet to win this season but scores high marks for his consistency over C&D this year (mainly in higher-grade company) and did well to finish fourth under Zac Purton here last month, having been hampered and denied a clear run; good chance.
0-6 in Hong Kong; kept on for respectable fifth over C&D on recent stable debut but double-digit draw makes things awkward today.

OwnerName: Siu Chak Wai, Siu Wai Chung & Siu Wing Kin
Spotlight: Disappointed when last in action in February, but was runner-up over C&D on two of his first three starts and remains lightly raced; contender if back on song after a break.

OwnerName: Clement Cheng Ka Man, Alfred Choi & Roger Li Ka Chun
Spotlight: Close third over C&D three weeks ago and has now made the frame on all five appearances; ought to play another major role from stall 2.
0-9 in Hong Kong; suggested he might be building up to something when fourth over C&D in April but two lesser efforts have followed.
Six-race maiden; dropped out tamely after making the running over 5f at Sha Tin last month, but made the frame on four of his five 6f starts; back up in trip today; considered each-way.

OwnerName: Tam Jin Rong & Zhao Xiao Na
Spotlight: Placed over C&D on five of his nine starts this season; below form here last month but recent 2lb drop is helpful; enters calculations.
Safely held on first two starts but belied 79-1 odds when fourth over C&D from stall 9 last month; much better drawn today; interesting to see whether he can build on that recent effort.

OwnerName: Edwin Fong Yick Chung, Edmund Ho Kwok Shing & So Wai Keung
Spotlight: Placed twice over C&D since his breakthrough win here in December, but is not making any progress and has a tough draw today; others preferred.
Placed over 5f in February but recent 6f form has been disappointing and he faces a tough task from stall 11 today.

OwnerName: Lam King Wai & Dr & Mrs Lam Kai Cheong
Spotlight: Unplaced all six starts but did pretty well to finish sixth under a positive ride from stall 14 at Sha Tin last month and has a much better draw here.

OwnerName: Lun Ka Leung & Yeung Hiu Yu
Spotlight: Consistent in Class 5 this season, but went up 7lb for last month's 5f win from stall 1 and has more on his plate in this higher grade.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 70,000gns yearling by Too Darn Hot; 8-1 when eighth of 12 at Newbury (6.5f, good to firm) on her introduction three weeks ago; that form's received a few boosts, however; tongue-tie goes on for her second start.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Bangkok; second foal; dam 6f winner (RPR 78), half-sister to winners Bear Faced (7f AW), Mercenary Rose (5f AW including 2yo) and Munificent (5f including AW), out of useful 6f-8.6f AW winner; not ruled out on pedigree but yard is 0-39 with juveniles over the last five years.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 110,000euros yearling by Blue Point; seventh foal; half-sister to winners Soldier's Empire (1m/1m2f including 2yo/AW/Bahrain; RPR 96), Mariesque (9.4f; 94), Green Soul (7f 2yo; 83) and Green Backspin (Scandinavian 6f-1m1f); dam French 7f (Listed) and 1m winner (99); respected yard among the winners and she needs monitoring in the market ahead of her debut.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By No Nay Never; third foal; dam unplaced, half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Midnight Mile, out of maiden close relative of Irish 1,000 Guineas winner Yesterday and Moyglare Stud winner Quarter Moon (both runner-up in Oaks); yard hasn't been firing on all cylinders with its juveniles but did have one run a screamer at Royal Ascot last week; betting can guide.

RaceName: Bet Smarter With Oddschecker+ EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Stepped it up on all three starts over 5f, culminating with solid third of eight in the Hilary Needler at Beverley (good to firm) 25 days ago; that wasn't the strongest renewal of that race but 6f should be no problem and she sets a clear standard; the one to beat.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Michael T H Lee, Vanessa Lee & Amelia Lee
Spotlight: Close second three consecutive times over C&D since March; ought to give another good account but wide draw makes things awkward today and Zac Purton has deserted him.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed over C&D on seasonal debut in September but both subsequent runs were disappointing, even if allowances are made for his wide draws; stall 9 is not ideal today; others have more pressing claims.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Just did enough to make all over 6f here in March; also suited by 5f and seemingly still in good form but needs to find something extra to overcome current mark.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: 25/26 Dennis C H Yip Trainer Syndicate (2)
Spotlight: 0-6 in Hong Kong but has made good late headway over C&D on last three appearances and has good claims under Zac Purton if he gets a strong pace to aim at.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
OwnerName: Chu Tak Wing, Casey Chu Yin Ling & Ronald Chu Tak Wah
Spotlight: Three C&D wins this year, most recently when running on well to collar Horsepower in this grade in March; not discredited from wide draw here last time, and remains of strong interest.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Four-time C&D winner; well below best form this season and has stall 12 to contend with today.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won readily over 6f at Sha Tin in January, his fourth Hong Kong start, but has not built on that success since and is not sure to be suited by this drop back to 5f; favourable draw not enough to tempt.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Down the field on first two Hong Kong starts but last month's C&D fifth was much more encouraging and he's better drawn today; not ruled out.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Placed over C&D in this higher grade since his two 6f Class 4 wins in March/April, but didn't fire when last in action and needs to bounce back with a career-best.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; launched Hong Kong career with two low-key C&D runs and is seemingly in need of significant further help from the local handicapper.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Scored off today's mark over C&D in December but recent efforts have been disappointing; needs to turn a corner.

RaceName: Des Voeux Road Central Handicap (Class 3) (Course A) (Turf)
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who won quite comfortably over the straight 5f at Sha Tin in October and has made the frame on three of his four subsequent 6f starts; could still have a bigger run in him back at 5f but wide draw dents his appeal.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Make Believe colt; 40,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Princess Mia (1m; RPR 67) and Wishforluck (Swedish 6f/7f including 2yo); dam French 1m4f winner, half-sister to winners Secret Existence (10.6f) and Twenty Times (1m2f), out of 1m2f/10.6f winner; stable has a good batch of 2yos and this one is respected on his debut.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Massaat; second foal; dam poor maiden (RPR 30), sister to French 1m4f winner Finjaans Rose, half-sister to winners Stepney (1m), Lady Charlie (7f 2yo) and Magical Flower (French 6.5f); already gelded and he doesn't feature among the leading newcomers on paper.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Blackbeard; 21,000euros foal, £35,000 yearling, 50,000euros breeze-up purchase in May; fourth foal; half-brother to winners Up The Anti (5.7f-1m; RPR 64) and Us Carmen (Polish 7f); dam unraced half-sister to 1m4f Listed winner Invite, out of 1m2f winner/Oaks third Katiyra; one of the more likely debutants.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: He rallied for third of nine in Doncaster maiden (5f, good to firm) in May and it was a solid effort up at 6f when 25-1 and fifth of 13 in the Woodcote at Epsom (good to soft) on Oaks day; already sets the standard and this new trip promises to unlock more.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Kameko; fifth foal; closely related to 7f winner Rousham (RPR 67), half-brother to winners First Of May (6f AW including 2yo; 85), Polka (5f AW; 72) and Zachary (5f/6f AW 2yo; 61); dam sprint maiden (55), out of five-time 1m Group 1 winner Soviet Song; interesting to see what the market makes of him on this debut.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: By Lope Y Fernandez; 35,000gns foal, 30,000gns yearling; first foal; dam twice-raced half-sister to useful winners Kings Gift (7f-1m2f including 2yo) and Queens Gift (5f/6f), out of 1m3f winner who stayed 2m; betting likely to provide some good clues on this introduction.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Half-brother to winners Amestris (5f 2yo; RPR 88) and Thisonesforyou (1m 2yo; 64); 25-1 when beaten just over 6l on his introduction here (6f, good) last Monday; worth a glance in the market but likely one for further down the line.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Promise at Bath (firm) on his April debut and again when second at Hamilton (6f, good) in May; wheels came off as favourite next time but that was only one week later on good to soft ground; cheekpieces go on and he could bounce back.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Rajasinghe filly; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Senor Carrots (5f AW; RPR 83) and Great Mates (8.6f AW; 81); dam 5f-1m winner (including 2yo; 94), sister to 6f 2yo winner Lady Katy, out of 5f/6f 2yo winner; yard has only sent out a handful of juveniles this term but is otherwise flying along of late; check the market.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 28-1 but she offered plenty to work on when fourth of ten at Leicester (7f, good) a fortnight ago; looked a touch raw on that first go and she's likely to improve with the experience under her belt; considered.

RaceName: Download The Oddschecker App EBF Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C/D) (Qualifier) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 50-1 when down the field over 6f at Redcar on last month's introduction; has plenty of winners in her pedigree but only of interest with market support.
Still 3lb higher than for C&D win in March, but was denied a clear run when sixth here three weeks ago and is not badly drawn today; each-way claims.
Largely consistent since narrow Sha Tin win in January and had stall 14 when only sixth there last month; much better drawn here and probably won't be far away.

Made all at Sha Tin in December and was placed off a mark 3lb higher than today's over C&D in March; slightly disappointing on latest outing in April but ideally drawn to attack from the front today; must be considered.
Progressive 4yo who has collected four C&D wins this season; still in good form, but not ideally drawn today and the handicapper may have his measure now.
Well beaten over 7f at Sha Tin last month and has now been unplaced all seven Hong Kong starts; can't be recommended from stall 12.
Seemed to have emerged from a dip in form when finishing well for fifth over C&D last month and remains on a workable mark, but others appeal more all the same.
Resurgent 6yo who has won his last two over C&D; can't be ruled out in current mood but combination of rise in grade and wide stall asks a lot of him today.
Won over 5f on debut in Australia last year and ran well from the front when fourth on recent Hong Kong debut at Sha Tin (6f); well drawn here; high on the list.
0-7 in Hong Kong but has twice gone close over C&D in recent months; usually ridden patiently; can make his presence felt again.
Fairly useful handicapper over 7f/1m when based in Ireland, but makes Hong Kong debut after nine-month break and looks opposable over this trip.

OwnerName: Arthur Antonio Da Silva, Betty Da Silva & Teresa Da Silva
Spotlight: Came good with two C&D wins in Class 4 in March/April; flopped badly when upped to this grade last month but further progress remains possible and Zac Purton keeps the faith.

Not an instant success in Hong Kong this year but was a clearcut C&D winner in April and his subsequent defeat from stall 12 is not viewed too harshly; may yet have more to offer but has another wide draw today.
Doubled her tally when winning at Thirsk (1m) in April; has continued to run with credit and it was a very solid fourth of 16 at York (1m1f, also good) 11 days ago; firmly in the picture again.

Won a tidy six-runner Epsom novice (7f) in September and he built on an encouraging comeback/handicap debut third (also good to soft) last month when going very close at Chester (7.5f, soft) 12 days ago; quicker ground raises a question but he's going the right way and must be factored in.

Ex-Irish runner who landed a Newcastle maiden (7f, AW) on his November stable debut; subsequently gelded and it was a very creditable fourth in a warm event at Ascot on his handicap debut last month; weakened to finish down the field in another competitive race next time but this represents a drop in grade and he shaped like this stiffer test would suit at Ascot.

Sole turf success came at Redcar (1m, good to firm) in August; he is now 16lb higher after a productive spell on AW and finished 12l adrift when returned to turf over C&D last month; down the list despite his yard being in fine form.
Showed a consistent level when placed on all three starts (7f/1m) on a variety of surfaces last year; enters handicaps on his reappearance having been gelded and this opening mark is probably fair.

Lightly raced 4yo who is yet to score but has shown ability across his six starts; latest third of 11 at Chester (7.5f, soft; acts on quicker) was a respectable effort and it's impossible to say he's not capable of better.

Four runs here have yielded two seconds and a win in this race in 2024; down the field in last year's renewal but after some modest efforts he's rediscovered his level of late, going close at Hamilton last month and finishing a close third at Ripon three weeks ago; yard going well and he's in the mix again.

Hit the ground running as a juvenile last term but her form tailed off at the backend and she hasn't rediscovered her best in four starts this year; did show a bit more on her last two starts, though, and her mark continues to come down.

Two AW wins for Hugo Palmer last season (1m, 8.5f); finished last on first two starts for new yard but a switch to blinkers prompted shock return to form when 80-1 and second of seven at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) last Monday; interesting to see if the aids work a second time.

Returned to form when 12-1 and prevailing by a nose at Ayr (1m, good to firm) last month; beaten just a neck off this mark at Hamilton last Wednesday and she's likely to play a part again with Tom Marquand taking the reins; due to go up 2lb.
Progressive 4yo who has collected two C&D wins this season and was placed over C&D on last two appearances; not ideally drawn today but still likely to be right in the thick of things again.
In good form over 6f/7f this season (three wins) and stays 1m perfectly well; back on turf after respectable dirt debut this month but current mark demands a career-best performance.

OwnerName: Mr & Mrs Kirk Wong King Wai
Spotlight: Won twice over C&D last season but has shown better form on dirt this term; needs to prove he can cope with a mark this high on turf.
Placed twice here in March (1m1f/1m) and is well handicapped on those efforts, but his form has dipped lately and younger rivals have more appealing profiles.
Game good in Hong Kong at fifth attempt, with C&D success last month; went up 6lb for that but has the inside draw again and Zac Purton keeps the ride; major player.

Has somewhat mixed record during this first season in Hong Kong, but came from off the pace to score over C&D last month and will presumably be dropped in again from his wide draw today; no surprise if he's on the premises.
Scored over C&D in Class 4 in November and was creditable second to Fivefortwo over C&D in this higher grade on stable debut in March; not seen since, though, and drawn much wider today.
Overcame wide draw to open Hong Kong account over C&D two weeks ago, his ninth attempt; much better drawn today but moves up in grade and Zac Purton evidently prefers Romantic Gladiator.
Won on dirt in early part of this season but has been badly out of form on turf in recent months; needs to turn things around.
Won three in a row from the front over C&D at start of this year and looked back in prime form when very close second over C&D three weeks ago; can make another bold bid.
Quite useful when trained in Britain but well down the field on first two Hong Kong starts (7f/1m).

OwnerName: Jessica Man Yi Lee & Gregory Chi Ho Ho
Spotlight: Ex-Australian 4yo; yet to make an impact after five Hong Kong starts (7f-1m) and seemingly in need of a drop in grade.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: She would have gone much closer with a clear run in a handicap at York (7f, good) last month and a return to front-running tactics bore fruit with a Listed win at Epsom (7f, good to soft) latest; below par on sole previous run on fast ground but she has leading form claims under a penalty.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Progressive since handicapping, placed twice in a row (6f/7f, good) before narrow win over this C&D (good to firm); others have stronger form but this well-bred sort could have plenty more left in the tank for her top yard.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: 2-3 in 2yo campaign and returned to form with close third to Albaydaa in C&D handicap (good to firm) last month; they reoppose on the same terms today and this lightly raced contender is open to further improvement, but she has something to find in today's company.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: She's had plenty of racing but has shown improvement on her last two runs, easily winning Class 4 handicap at Lingfield (7f, good to firm) before fifth of sixth to Ellusive Butterfly (who is now penalised) in Epsom Listed race (7f, good to soft), beaten about 4l; on the up and has each-way claims.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Strong form when winning Doncaster maiden (7f, good) last September on second start; disappointing since, including in Listed race at Goodwood (1m2f, good) on last month's reappearance, and has something to prove, but she's not written off back down in trip in first-time blinkers.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Won on debut at Southwell (7f, AW) in April and close second in Redcar novice (6f, good to firm) later that month; she should have a bright future but considerable improvement is needed if she's to threaten today.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Listed runner-up last October (6f, heavy) and kept on for fourth of 15 in Listed race at Haydock (6f, good to soft) last month, providing optimism that she would be suited by 7f; however, she hasn't seen out 7f either side of that Haydock run, most recently sixth of seven at Musselburgh (good).

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: 0-5 in Group/Listed races since winning last July on first two starts but she kept on well for a Listed third at Musselburgh (7f, good) last time and today's stiffer track could be right up her street; one to consider.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: She's raced nine times and produced her best RPR yet when close second in C&D handicap (good to firm) last month, splitting Albaydaa and Applesandpears who were carrying 2lb more; probably vulnerable back up in grade.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Eternal Stakes (Listed Race) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Won at Newbury (7f, good to firm) last June on sole 2yo start and down the field in the Group 3 Fred Darling there (7f, good) on reappearance; beaten only about 3l when sixth of eight in Listed race at Chantilly (1m, good to soft) last month and shaped as though the return to 7f would help; her stable is in excellent form and she might have an each-way shout.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Made it 2-3 at Musselburgh when winning there over 7f in April off a 1lb lower mark than today's; beaten only 4l when seventh of 19 at York (7f, good) 11 days ago but stamina has to be a concern on just his third try over a 1m.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner who has won four times since blinkers were added (7f/1m), most recently over 7f at Chester last month off 3lb lower; didn't get home there over 1m2f (good to soft) 11 days ago but should be happier back at this trip.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Six-time winner, the most recent coming on the AW at Southwell off this mark in November; runner-up off a 4lb lower mark in this race in 2024 but he hasn't threatened in three starts this year; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Promise in three starts for the Gosden stable and he caught the eye when third of 15 at York (1m2f, good) on last month's stable debut; again shaped promisingly at Hamilton (8.5f, good) next time, despite the steady gallop not suiting, but was disappointing back at York 12 days ago, albeit he may have not been helped by ending up towards the far rail; drops in grade and he's the type to bounce back if getting this run to suit.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Added to his two AW successes since joining these connections when winning over this C&D 25 days ago (good to firm); best form has been on quick ground and he's a contender off this 3lb higher mark; cheekpieces, worn once previously, now return.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Prominent racer (can front run) who won three times last year, twice at Doncaster (1m) and at York (1m, good); reappeared from 203 days off with a solid effort at Newmarket (1m, good) last month but he was withdrawn after becoming upset in the stalls at Doncaster on his last intended start.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Won a maiden over 1m at two but all four wins since have been over shorter, the most recent at Beverley (7.5f, good to firm) last month; too free at York (7f) 11 days ago and he'll need to settle better now returned to this trip with the hood back on (worn once in 2023).

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Won four times last year (7f/7.5f) and also posted some other good efforts, including when third of 21 in a competitive handicap at York (7f, good) in October; didn't settle well enough at Chelmsford (7f, AW) in November when last seen but the break may have done her some good.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Heavy-ground maiden winner at Listowel in September when trained by Paddy Twomey; well beaten in the Spring Mile on his yard debut but his two efforts since (1m2f) have been a bit better; cheekpieces now go on but he wouldn't want the ground too lively.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Often front-runner who can race freely; sole win from ten starts came in a Ripon novice (1m, good) in August 2024 but his best effort since undergoing wind surgery came when a 1l third of 11 at Nottingham (8.5f, good) last month; below his best in two starts since.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Hold-up performer whose record on turf reads 1-17 compared to 6-43 on the AW; shaped as if still in form when sixth of 11 at Lingfield (7f, AW) 117 days ago but may need her first outing since.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Can front-run and acts on most ground; most recent of his three wins came here on soft ground over 1m1f last June and he's been in fine form this year, finishing runner-up in each of his three starts; once again has each-way claims.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Creditable sixth of 16 at York (1m1f, good) before winning at Nottingham (8.5f, good) under Jake Dickson four days ago, making it two wins from his last three starts; escapes a penalty for that success and can't be dismissed.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win since joining this yard came in a first-time visor at Wolverhampton (8.5f, AW) in December and he's ran some good races since the cheekpieces went on; didn't get the best of passages at Wetherby (1m, good to firm) nine days ago and can be treated as if still in form; up in grade but could go well.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from 22 starts came at Cork in April 2023; fourth over hurdles at Perth before finishing second of ten behind an improving filly at Ayr (1m2f, soft) five days ago; drops back in trip with place claims.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Carlisle Bell Handicap
OwnerName: S Bulmer, P Hebdon & R Taylor
Spotlight: All three wins came in 2024 over 1m2f (good/good to firm) but he was runner-up three times last season; fair effort at York last time and now has first-time blinkers replacing cheekpieces now trying 1m for the first time since his debut.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Ex-French soft-ground winner who had been gelded when 50-1 and a solid fourth at Chester (10.3f, good) on his stable debut last month; outpaced over 1m2f on a fast surface next time so this stiffer test should suit and Tom Marquand takes over.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Fairly unexposed sort who showed his best form when taking 14-runner Ascot handicap (1m4f, good to firm) seven weeks ago; that race has worked out well, with the third and fourth winning big handicaps at Royal Ascot and at Epsom on Derby day respectively, so a 5lb rise now looks on the lenient side; form of his close second on final start of last year was also boosted at last week's royal meeting and he's a key contender.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Two 1m4f wins for Ben Brookhouse on a sound surface but he finished well adrift on his reappearance and first start since wind surgery at Nottingham in April; much more competitive when fifth of 15 at Chester (12.5f, good) one month later; was well drawn that day but it's still early days for this yard.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 2-2 as a juvenile but he didn't progress at three (quite highly tried) and left Karl Burke for 25,000gns that October; missed last year but there was plenty of encouragement when fourth at Chester (10.5f, good to soft) 11 days ago; fairly quick second run back from a long absence sounds a note of caution but his yard's in fine form; respected.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Seven-time winner whose three appearances here have yielded two wins (7f and 1m) back in 2022/2023; also finished runner-up in this race (good to firm) in 2024 and he was back on track when fairly close third of four at Hamilton (1m5f, good to firm) four weeks ago; winner and second have won subsequently and he warrants consideration.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Made it win number six when scoring here (1m6f, good; has won at up to 2m2f) last Monday; 5lb penalty makes life tough and he's never won at shorter than 1m6f; evidently in good heart, though.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Dual Flat winner for Harry Eustace (1m2f, good) who also scored twice over hurdles for Jedd O'Keeffe; became very inconsistent for latter yard, however, and he was picked up for £20,000 one month ago; refitted tongue-tie and first-time cheekpieces for this stable debut.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Not the force of old but, after a couple of modest efforts, his latest fifth of 18 at York (10.5f, good; first-time cheekpieces) was a step back in the right direction; shaped as though this stiffer test might suit on that occasion so interesting to see if the headgear works a second time for in-form yard.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Showed ability in Irish maidens for Noel Meade but yet to get going in two starts for this yard, latterly in a hood (now removed) at Thirsk last month; this is just her third handicap but it's a tough one in which to stage a revival.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Seemingly exposed gelding who has suddenly got on a roll since going back up in trip, scoring on good ground at Hamilton (1m3f), Musselburgh (12.5f) and over C&D last month; has to defy a further 4lb rise in a better race from the widest stall but he can't be ignored attempting a four-timer.

RaceName: Stablemate By Agma Cumberland Plate Handicap (GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Middleham Park Racing Xxiii & M Hulin
Spotlight: He has been doing well since being gelded and tackling handicaps on a sound surface, winning four-runner event at Leicester (1m4f) and three-runner race at Redcar (1m6f); far from disgraced in a Class 2 at Doncaster last time, bringing this year's form figures to 21213; not fully exposed and likely capable of better yet.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister by Cotai Glory to a 6f/7f winner Kisskodi; dam 7f AW winner, half-sister to 6f Listed winner Castle Hill Cassie.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Fifth foal; Nando Parrado filly is a sister to winners in Spain and Italy; dam placed 1m2f 2yo, half-sister to Group 3 winners Abjer and Gloomy Sunday; fair pedigree but not a likely debut winner in a maiden of this standard.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Well beaten in third on 5f Dundalk debut; out of her depth in 6f Curragh Group 3 last month; still looked a bit raw when fifth of 17 in a recent 6f maiden at Fairyhouse, but that was an encouraging display nonetheless.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Just touched off in an early-season 5f maiden at Cork; winner Velozee won her next race and has confirmed a fair standard of form in Pattern races won by Victorious, fourth at this venue and eighth in last week's Queen Mary; a leading contender.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Miss Kate Harrington & Mrs John Harrington & B Phelan
Spotlight: Second in a seven-runner maiden over C&D on debut; enough merit in her Curragh fourth (behind the Albany Stakes winner Sun Goddess) to suggest she can go close.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly was not particularly strong in the market when a 5l third of eight for her debut at Listowel (6.5f, good); dam is by Galileo, so some reservations about the suitability of the trip; her brother Amadeus Mozart was second in last week's Golden Gate Handicap (1m2f); still, could pose a lively threat.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Failed to sell at 220,000gns at Newmarket last October; fifth foal; half-sister by Blackbeard to winners Awaken (Group-placed 6f 2yo and Alrahy (KSA 1m); dam 1m2f AW winner, half-sister to multiple 1m2f Group 1 winner Twice Over; probably best watched on debut.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 55,000gns Y (n/s 75,000gns 2yo); first foal; unraced dam closely related to useful winner First Winter, half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Aesop's Fables (Prix Jean Prat), out of useful winning close relative of Derby/Arc winner Lammtarra.

RaceName: Dooley Insurance Irish EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Made an encouraging debut over 5f at Cork last month; failed to build on that in a 6f Fairyhouse race nine days later.

OwnerName: J Fyffe, A Edmondson & L McGurty
Spotlight: Mostly a hold-up mare; two wins from just five starts here feature a success over this trip (soft) two years ago and another over 6f (good to firm) last May; on her third trainer when scoring over 1m at Newcastle (AW) in January but she'll need to be better than ever to defy this mark on her first start since February; this could set up quite nicely, though.

Took 17 goes to strike for this yard but it all came together at Ripon (1m, good) three weeks ago; will need extra up 3lb but that was the first time she sported this headgear combination and today's stiff 7f is probably not a problem.

Went fairly close on her debut over C&D in August and she made all for an easy Catterick maiden (6f, also good) win three weeks later; came up short in Group 3 and Listed company subsequently but cheekpieces go on and she's impossible to rule out ahead of this reappearance/handicap debut; watch the market.

Both starts at 7f; encouraging debut when second of five at Haydock (soft) in May and she was away briskly when making all at Yarmouth (good to soft) a fortnight ago; that was a ready win and this better ground ought not to be a concern on pedigree; key contender on her handicap debut.

Got off the mark on her AW reappearance in April and following a solid handicap debut she struck at Wetherby (also 7f, good to firm; made all) three weeks ago; received only a 1lb nudge for that neck success and there's a good chance we've yet to see the best of her.

15-race maiden who went very close over C&D just over a year ago; finished down the field in this race (also soft) nine days later but was back in some form from August following wind surgery; reappearance sixth in a blanket finish at Hamilton last month was a solid effort but slow starts have become a theme and she's 2lb 'wrong'.
Dual winner over this trip in 2024; lost his form soon afterwards and has been absent almost a year so is best watched unless market support comes.
Finally got off the mark at Navan over this trip last time with cheekpieces replacing other headgear; was a cosy winner then and should go well even off 8lb higher with a visor back on.
Thed last of his nine wins came over 6f at this track last year; has been disappointing in two starts this year but is now 7lb below that last winning mark; needs a return to form.
Thed last of his two wins came at Bellewstown in 2023; some decent runs in defeat this year but was poor at Fairyhouse last time and has to rebound.
Generally regressive in the last while and was poor in a Fairyhouse claimer last time in a first-time visor; others make more appeal.
Good runs in defeat over this trip at Dundalk and Down Royal lately but was below that level over 6f at Fairyhouse; respected back to 5f.
Yet to win but his three best runs have come on AW; not bad runs at Navan over 6f-5f last twice but needs more down 2lb.
Some decent runs in defeat on AW over 5f-6f during the winter but was poor at Fairyhouse on recent return; has to improve hugely on that.
Got off the mark at Tipperary last August over this trip; some solid efforts since including on comeback behind Happy Henry at Navan; can improve from that and is in the mix.
Seven wins (incl' at this track) between turf and AW over 5f-6f for former yard; not a bad stable debut at Fairyhouse last time and can be in the mix with some improvement.
Maiden has been a close runner-up in three of his last four starts between 5f-6f; an extra 3lb for his Fairyhouse second doesn't help but should be involved again.
Sole win came on AW at Newcastle over 6f last winter; hit and miss since and has to rebound from a poor Fairyhouse effort.
Maiden anticipated the start at Cork last time and was always in rear; can do better but looks the yard's second-string behind Happy Henry.
Well beaten in handicaps at Dundalk and the Curragh lately so has to find improvement back to 5f.
Not a bad effort at Down Royal over 5f but wasn't as good over 6f at Fairyhouse since; dropped 3lb so mightn't be far away back to this trip.
Third runner for yard; best form has come over this trip on soft ground, winning at Bellewstown last autumn; fine efforts back there and at Cork this season; drying ground isn't in her favour.
All three wins came when trained in UK; has been below form for this yard and has to stage a revival even from a dropping mark.

OwnerName: Ms Yvonne Hennessy & Ms S O'Neill
Spotlight: Well beaten lately and officially rated only 31 so not easy to fancy.
Similar efforts in defeat both starts this year; travelled well at Fairyhouse last time over 6f but didn't find a lot off the bridle; coming back to this trip might help.
Best effort came over this trip at Down Royal last year; poor over 7f and 6f both runs this year; has to do much better but coming back to 5f might help.
Best efforts this year have come over this trip on soft ground, including at Down Royal last time from 7lb out of the handicap; only 2lb 'wrong' today but the drying ground might not suit as well.

RaceName: Race & Stay Irish Racing Tours Rated Race
Spotlight: Made the frame in three races at the Curragh last season, all won by Ballydoyle-trained colts; most meaningful form when third of five in a 7f Group 2 won by Constitution River; odds-on for a comfortable 7f maiden win at Gowran on reappearance; failed to stay, and outclassed in any case in the Irish 2,000 Guineas; best of these, but hard to be sure that he has the requisite speed.

RaceName: Race & Stay Irish Racing Tours Rated Race
Spotlight: Two wins last season, including a 6f Dundalk event in which today's rival Amiata was the unplaced favourite; kept on for fourth place in a C&D handicap on seasonal debut; back to 6f here after a nondescript run when favourite for a 7f handicap at the Curragh.

RaceName: Race & Stay Irish Racing Tours Rated Race
Spotlight: Stable second-string when landing a 7f maiden at Fairyhouse on his third start; may continue on the upgrade but this is a tough task against three more experienced rivals.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Irish EBF Jannah Rose Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
OwnerName: M Tabor & D Smith & Mrs J Magnier & Westerberg
Spotlight: Curragh 7f maiden winner at two (soft to heavy); took a big step forward with a convincing win in the Listed Oaks Trial at Lingfield (good to firm) last month on her sixth start, and her first run beyond 1m; appeared to have a good chance of staying the Oaks trip, but faded from over two furlongs down; will be hard to beat if the race has not left a mark.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Irish EBF Jannah Rose Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Winner and Listed-placed over 7f at two; hooded when disappointing in Guineas trial over that trip on comeback; made some late progress in a 1m Group 3 at the Curragh; much-improved only five days later when beating La Fogata in a Listed contest over C&D.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Irish EBF Jannah Rose Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Kempton maiden winner at two; beaten in a four-runner handicap at Wolverhampton; had shown better form in making the frame in two Listed races over 1m2f on turf, both at Goodwood; hard to see her winning at this level.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Irish EBF Jannah Rose Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Useful form over 1m as a juvenile; beaten 5l in a six-runner Curragh Group 3, won a Punchestown maiden; heavy ground a possible excuse for a poor run on final start for Ger Lyons; finished in front of well-fancied stablemate Eretria when second to Caught U Sleeping (levels) over C&D on first outing for this team; another solid run at Navan last time.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Irish EBF Jannah Rose Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Won 7f Curragh maiden on heavy in March; ran green and was blocked in her run when starting to stay on from rear in the last quarter-mile of a 1m Group 3 event at the same venue early last month; reappeared in a C&D Listed race five days later, kept on in fourth, needs to reverse form with Caught U Sleeping and La Fogata to win here.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Irish EBF Jannah Rose Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Lope De Vega filly cost 500,000gns as a yearling; made a promising debut when winning a maiden over the extended 1m1f at Gowran in March; especially good effort considering she was the stable's second-string; beaten 2 3/4l in third was her short-priced stablemate Johanna Walsh, who went close in last week's Ribblesdale; promising filly.

RaceName: Al Shira'aa Racing Irish EBF Jannah Rose Stakes (Group 3) (Fillies)
Spotlight: Gowran maiden winner; creditable fourth in the 1m Group 3 Athasi Stakes at the Curragh, with Caught U Sleeping and Pollenca among those behind; out of her depth in the Irish 1,000 Guineas; useful form, but ominous that the champion jockey deserts her to ride the once-raced Rebel Moon
The latest of her five wins came over 1m4f at Roscommon in May; unseated early next time and 1m1.5f looked too short last time at Gowran; has to rebound.
The last of his four wins came three years ago; not a bad fifth at Leopardstown last time in a higher grade than this; needs more but shouldn't be far away.
Won off 2lb lower at Navan last year but lost his form badly at the end of last season; needs a return to his best after time away.
Maiden hasn't had many runs for his age, five of them over hurdles; not bad efforts lately after an absence in a Ballinrobe maiden and Limerick claimer; can be a player.
Decent efforts in defeat on AW last twice between 1m2f-1m4f; has a chance on handicap debut after a break if as effective on turf.
The second of her two wins last year came over C&D; was just denied at Leopardstown but has to rebound from a lesser Fairyhouse run.
Won over 1m at Gowran last year on heavy ground but handles better too; stays this trip but has been hit and miss this year so needs his best.
Leopardstown winner last year off 58 for former yard was just denied at Gowran last time, his best effort since; should go well off 1lb higher.
Won by 5l at Navan last autumn and basically struggled off higher marks since; been hurdling lately and needs more off this mark.
Maiden has posted some decent efforts in defeat including at Fairyhouse last time over 1m2f; can be involved with the tongue-tie added then retained.
Won over this trip on AW in March wearing first-time blinkers but has been poor twice since so has questions to answer now.
Put in decent efforts on both handicap starts over similar trips to this, at Roscommon and Down Royal; just bumped into a well-handicapped rival last time but can be a player again.
Was well beaten in three Flat maidens in 2024; over hurdles since, winning a Kilbeggan handicap last August; others appeal more.
Narrowly got off the mark on handicap debut at Gowran last time, beating today's rival Neon Eyes a head over 1m1.5f; cheekpieces added and raised 7lb but should stay this trip so respected.
Built upon her Gowran handicap debut over 1m when just denied by Amrum over 1m1.5f at the same track last time; 2lb better off with that rival today so is a player.
Just glimpses of ability in three maidens and has to raise his game even dropping to handicaps; worth a market look, all the same.
Beaten a long way in three maidens and can only be watched with a view to handicaps; blinkers added.

Price rose from E42,000 as a yearling to E50,000 as a breeze-up 2yo; third foal; half-brother to winners Desdemona (5f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 79) and Amroon (6f/7f AW inc 2yo; 63); dam 5f-7f winner (inc 2yo; 83); has taken some time to get to the track; best watched unless strong in the market

Fifth foal; dam 7f-11.6f winner (inc Listed; RPR 102), out of unraced sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Meath; dam is yet to breed a winner even though has visited some top sires; best watched unless the market suggests otherwise.
Beaten 10l on debut at Limerick five days ago when 200/1; can do better in time but still best watched.
Finished a close second on debut over 7f at Limerick but didn't build on that over a mile at Killarney since when raced keenly; has to resume progress but yard is in form after two Royal Ascot wins.
Always in rear in his three maiden runs and will be of more interest in handicaps.

Second foal; dam unraced half-sister to 1m2f/11.6f Listed winner Flying Fairies, out of unraced sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Meath; one to watch for future reference unless attracting market support.

OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Largely consistent in defeat in four maidens under varying ground conditions over similar trips to this; was a head behind Saliba at Gowran last time and will have to find improvement for being gelded since.
Has posted solid runs in defeat in five starts up to a mile; hung when under pressure at Leopardstown last time and blinkers are added today; needs to find a bit more to win but has to be considered.

Took a rating of 95 into this season after finishing 6.75l behind Christmas Day in a Leopardstown Group 3 on final 2yo start; hasn't run to that level in both starts this season; still top-rated in this but vulnerable too; looks stable first-string.
The best of two maiden runs at the Curragh was last time over 6.5f but needs loads more to get involved today.

Sixth foal; dam unplaced 5f 2yo (RPR 55), half-sister to 6f 2yo Listed winner Hold Your Colour, out of 7f 2yo winner; by a dual-purpose sire; a E2,000 vendor buyback as a foal; best watched on debut for future reference.

Fifth foal; closely related to 7f-1m4f winner Golden Lyra (inc 2yo/AW/Listed; RPR 109), half-brother to winners incl' Going Gone (1m2f/1m4f; 103); cost E75,000 as a yearling; dam unraced sister to German Derby winner Sea The Moon; lots to like on paper and one to consider if strong in the market.

Caught the eye on debut when running on late over this trip at Gowran, a head in front of Kalir; the second home has won since and this gelding should have made progress too; yard is in form so should be in the mix.

OwnerName: Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor
Spotlight: Third foal; dam 1m4f Oaks winner (RPR 117), sister to 1m 2yo Group 1 winner Together Forever (dam of City Of Troy), half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Lord Shanakill, out of unraced half-sister to US 1m1f Grade 1 winner Spanish Fern; fantastic pedigree and needs a market check on debut; tongue-tie.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Nothing in her three starts suggests any prospect of featuring in a maiden.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Frankel filly started her career with a fifth placing in a 1m Leopardstown maiden in which the subsequent Oaks winner Thundering On finished second; again finished fifth in a 1m2f maiden at the same venue on second start, beaten 9 1/2l behind the Ribblesdale runner-up Johanna Walsh; tongue-tie fitted now.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: 200-1 chance when finishing at the back over 7f at Fairyhouse; can be ruled out.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Mrs John Magnier & Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith
Spotlight: Started slowly and never a threat at Dundalk on only juvenile start; better when fifth behind the talented Sparan Nua on reappearance over C&D; Beatific appears to give the stable a better chance.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Wootton Bassett filly made some late headway on debut at Leopardstown; perhaps a little unlucky not to peg back a front-running 50-1 chance over C&D last month; effective at this trip but bred to stay further; cheekpieces fitted.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Respectable fourth of 12 on her Cork debut last month (7f, soft to heavy); more like one for handicaps after her recent Leopardstown run.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Made no impression first time out at Leopardstown; unlikely to feature.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Mid-field over this trip at Gowran on debut; may need further than this to prosper.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: E5,000 yearling; second foal; Circus Maximus filly whose dam won over 6f in France, sister to a 1m winner Dilmun, half-sister to 1m 2yo Group 3 winner Liquid Amber; best watched.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Had a run in a barrier trial last year; shaped promisingly when third behind the smart Sparan Nua (now unbeaten in three) in a C&D maiden last month; 40-1 then, and was only 2 1/4l behind her odds-on stablemate Cromac Quay; should make her presence felt

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: In rear in 6f maidens at Fairyhouse and Naas last July; no better over 7f on reappearance.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Ninth foal; half-sister by Soldier's Call to 1m AW winner Yimkin (RPR 74); dam 6f 2yo winner (91), half-sister to 1m1f Group 1 winner Olden Times and 6f Listed winner Festoso, out of Group-placed 6f winner.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Not a big price in her first two races, needs to improve in first-time cheekpieces.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Towards the back in company with Lecale Breeze first time out at Leopardstown.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: First foal; dam placed 7f-1m6f (inc 2yo; RPR 63), half-sister to winners Albaflora (1m4f Listed), Alyssa (14.5f Group 2), Aloe Vera (1m2f Listed) and Alea Iacta (1m 2yo Group 3), out of once-raced half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Alla Speranza.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan
Spotlight: Sixth foal; half-sister by Night Of Thunder to winners Sumiha (1m2f/1m4f inc Group 3; RPR 108) and Suwayra (1m 2yo; 91); dam Group-placed 1m2f winner (95), half-sister to 1m Group 3 winner Sinawann and 2m Listed winner Silwana, out of half-sister to Derby/Arc winner Sinndar.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Down the field in maidens at Cork and Gowran, not likely to feature.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Two runs at Dundalk last winter; fine effort on debut when third over 7f, slowly away and not getting a clear run before staying on; again met interference on second start, and was beaten 2l in third behind a longer-priced stablemate.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Twice placed over 6f on AW late last year; fourth in 5f Cork maiden on reappearance; never in contention on handicap debut over 6f at this venue; better when stepped up to 7f in a Down Royal maiden; third then, but will have to make further progress to reach the money here.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Placed in all four maiden attempts, including third behind stablemate Drop Dead Gorgeous at Naas in March; subsequently last of 12 in a Group 3 event at the Curragh and unplaced in a handicap at the same track; prominent for a long way in a Listed race at Navan last time, could feature in this company if first-time blinkers have a positive effect.
